hints: A scalar subquery is a query in parentheses that returns exactly one value. You can drop it anywhere SQL expects a value — including on the right side of the > in your WHERE clause.
Replace the literal 60000 with (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employee). The database runs that inner query first, gets the average, then filters as if you'd typed that number.
Full shape: SELECT first_name, salary FROM employee WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employee) ORDER BY salary DESC; The average is 65625, so only Jan and Michael clear it.
